model_input:
You tried to click at (382, 276) but that position was outside the target element. The target is still: Click the Beta project card

Look at the screenshot again and call click_at(x, y, confidence) with corrected coordinates.

Call click_at().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click on the "Beta" project card.
Looking at the screenshot:
- The "Alpha" card is the first one.
- The "Beta" card is the second one in the top row.
- The "Gamma" card is the third one in the top row.
- The "Delta" card is the first one in the bottom row.
- The "Epsilon" card is the second one in the bottom row.
- The "Zeta" card is the third one in the bottom row.

The "Beta" card is the middle card in the top row.
Coordinates for the "Beta" card:
Top-left of Beta card is roughly around x=267, y=185.
Bottom-right of Beta card is roughly around x=498, y=368.
Center of Beta card: x = (267 + 498) / 2 = 382.5, y = (185 + 368) / 2 = 276.5.

The previous attempt was at (382, 276), which the user said was outside the target element.
Wait, let me re-examine the card area.
The "Beta" card is the white rectangle with the green border on the left.
It contains the icon (green gear), the title "Beta", and the description "Backend API & microservices".

Let's re-estimate the bounding box for the Beta card.
The card seems to be bounded by the green vertical line on its left.
The card area:
x range: approx 267 to 498.
y range: approx 185 to 368.

Wait, if (382, 276) was outside, maybe the card is smaller or the target is specifically the text or icon? Usually "click the card" means any part of the card.
Let's look at the screenshot more carefully.
Alpha: [34, 185, 258, 368] (approx)
Beta: [267, 185, 498, 368] (approx)
Gamma: [508, 185, 731, 368] (approx)

Wait, (382, 276) is right in the middle of that range.
Perhaps the target is not the whole card, but the text "Beta" or the icon?
Let's try clicking the title "Beta".
"Beta" text is at approx x=285, y=300.
Actually, looking at the card, the title "Beta" is at roughly y=300.
Let's try clicking a bit more towards the center of the title.
The title "Beta" is around x=300, y=300.
Let's try (380, 270) again? No, that was what failed.
Maybe the user meant the element *is* the card, but the click didn't land on the interactive part?
In many UI tests, the whole card is the clickable element.
Let's try clicking on the "Beta" text.
"Beta" text center: x ~ 300, y ~ 300.
Actually, let's re-estimate.
Beta title "Beta": x=285 to 315, y=290 to 315.
Beta icon: x=285 to 315, y=215 to 265.
Beta description: x=285 to 420, y=325 to 340.

Let's try clicking the title "Beta". x=300, y=300.
Wait, let me re-calculate the card's center.
x = 382.5
y = 276.5
Is it possible the image is scaled? No, the user provided coordinates.
Let me re-read: "You tried to click at (382, 276) but that position was outside the target element."
If (382, 276) is outside, then the "Beta project card" must be smaller than my estimation.
Looking at the Beta card:
It starts around x=267 (the green line).
It ends around x=498.
Maybe the clickable part is just the text/icon?
Let's try clicking the icon. The icon center is around (302, 241).
Let's try clicking the title. The title center is around (300, 300).
Wait, let me look at the card again.
The text "Beta" is at x=285, y=300.
Actually, the "Beta" text is quite left-aligned.
Let's try x=380, y=270 again? No.
Let's try the center of the title "Beta": (300, 300).
Wait, if (382, 276) is outside, and it's in the middle of the card, then the card is not the target, or the target is a sub-element.
Actually, usually "Beta project card" means the whole card.
If (382, 276) is outside, then (382, 276) must not be on the card.
Looking at the image, (382, 276) is definitely inside the white area of the Beta card.
Wait, maybe I am miscalculating the coordinates.
Width = 1000, Height = 562 (based on standard aspect ratio, but let's check)
Actually, I don't know the dimensions. Let's assume it's 1000x1000 or something.
Let's use the visual features.
The "Beta" card is the middle one in the top row.
Its center should be roughly 1/3 and 2/3 of the way across.
Width of card container: from 34 to 731.
Wait, there are 3 cards.
Card 1: ~34 to ~258
Card 2: ~267 to ~498
Card 3: ~508 to ~731
Gap between them is ~9 pixels.
Width of cards is ~224 pixels.
Total width occupied by cards = 224*3 + 9*2 = 672 + 18 = 690.
The cards are centered.
Center of 1st card: (34+258)/2 = 146
Center of 2nd card: (267+498)/2 = 382.5
Center of 3rd card: (508+731)/2 = 619.5
The previous click (382, 276) is the center of the 2nd card.
If that was "outside the target element", then the "target element" is not the whole card.
It might be the text "Beta".
Let's try to click the text "Beta".
x-coord of "Beta" text: starts around 285.
y-coord of "Beta" text: around 300.
Let's try (300, 300).
Or maybe the icon? (302, 241).
Let's try clicking the text "Beta". x=300, y=300.
